_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about what local government does and how people in the community use and help with local services.

_source.w: We are learning about what local government does and how people in the community use and help with local services.
y: Local government provides services we use every day, like libraries and rubbish collection.
Understanding local government helps us know who to ask when something in our area needs fixing.
Learning how people contribute to local services shows us that communities work best when everyone helps.
eg: Finding out who runs our local library, collects our bins, and maintains our parks.
Investigating how local councillors are chosen and what they do.
Visiting a local council website to see the services available in our area.
Discussing how volunteers help with community events like clean-up days.
Looking at how a new playground or footpath gets planned and built by the council.
Writing a letter to the local council about something we think could be improved.
